articles.title: Life in Tehran: People Hope for Peace or War?
articles.summary: In Tehran, Iran, a man named Mohammad works in his family's shoe shop. He says many people have lost their jobs because of the war and because the government turned off the internet. His father Mustafa has owned the shop for 40 years. They sell shoes made in China. Even fake shoes are expensive. An old woman named Shahla buys bread. She says bread costs three times more now. People are struggling to pay for food. Some people hope the war will start again because they think things might get better after the war. Others are tired of the bad economy. The street has shops selling food, flowers, and household goods. Life is hard for people in Tehran, and they don't know what will happen next.

articles.title: Police on Beaches to Stop Boat Crossings
articles.summary: The UK and France have made a new agreement to stop people from crossing the English Channel in small boats. The UK will give France £662 million over three years. France will send at least 50 riot-trained police to beaches and use drones, helicopters, and cameras to stop illegal crossings. Some of the money may be taken back if not enough crossings are stopped. Many people try to cross the Channel to reach the UK because they think life will be better there. In 2025, over 41,000 people arrived in the UK by small boat. Some UK politicians say France should do more to stop the boats, but France says it is already working hard.

articles.title: Girl Finds Rare Salamander Under Bridge
articles.summary: A 10-year-old girl named Evie found a rare Mexican axolotl under a bridge in Wales. An axolotl is a type of salamander that lives in water. Evie was playing in the river when she saw the axolotl hiding under a mat. The axolotl, named Dippy, was hurt and had damage to its tail and stomach. Evie and her mom took Dippy home to care for it. Experts say this is the first time an axolotl has been found in the wild in the UK. Axolotls are endangered, with only 50 to 1,000 left in the world. They have become popular as pets because of video games like Minecraft. Evie loves nature and often finds bugs and newts. Her mom was very surprised by the discovery. The family cut their holiday short to take Dippy to their home in Leicester. A reptile expert said Evie probably saved Dippy's life.

articles.title: Shops on UK High Streets Selling Illegal Drugs
articles.summary: BBC secret filming found that some shops on UK High Streets are selling illegal drugs like cocaine and cannabis. Researchers went into shops in four towns in the West Midlands and were able to buy drugs easily. One street was called 'lawless' by a police source. The National Crime Agency says organized criminal gangs are using these shops to sell drugs. A senior MP wants the government to act quickly to stop this. The government says it is working with police to take action against these criminal businesses. Legitimate businesses nearby said they have been scared by gangs and have seen violence with knives and guns.

articles.title: UK Says No to Cigarettes for Kids Born After 2008
articles.summary: The UK Parliament passed a new law that will stop children born after December 31, 2008 from ever buying cigarettes. This means that as these kids grow up, they will never be allowed to buy cigarettes, even when they become adults. The law also gives the government power to control how tobacco, vapes, and nicotine products are sold, including their flavors and packaging. Currently, it is illegal to sell cigarettes to people under 18, but this new law makes it so that the age to buy cigarettes goes up every year, so younger generations will never be old enough to buy them. The number of people who smoke in the UK has gone down a lot since the 1970s, but about 6.4 million people still smoke. Smoking causes about 80,000 deaths each year in the UK and is the number one cause of preventable death. The goal is to create a smoke-free generation and protect children from the harms of smoking.

articles.title: Search for Missing Crew After Ship Overturns in Typhoon
articles.summary: A cargo ship called the Mariana overturned near the Northern Mariana Islands during a powerful typhoon. The ship had six crew members on board. After the storm, searchers found the body of one crew member. They are still looking for the other five. The searchers used a special underwater drone and divers to check inside the ship. They also looked for a life raft that might have floated away. The typhoon, named Sinlaku, was very strong with winds up to 150 miles per hour. Many people from different countries helped in the search, covering an area as big as the state of Oregon. The ship had lost one of its engines during the storm and asked for help, but then contact was lost. Everyone hopes the missing crew will be found safe.
